description: Nintendo's legendary action-adventure series spanning 40 years of Hyrule, dungeons, and the eternal struggle between Link, Zelda, and Ganon(dorf). One of the most critically acclaimed franchises in gaming history, with entries across every Nintendo platform and a timeline so complex Nintendo published an official book to explain it.

media.title: The Legend of Zelda
media.description: The original — top-down open-world adventure that defined the genre. Find all eight dungeons in any order.
media.notes: NES version or FDS original. GBA port also playable.

media.title: Zelda II: The Adventure of Link
media.description: The black sheep side-scroller. RPG levelling, towns, and brutal difficulty. Unique in the series.
media.notes: Optional — most skip it. Worth trying for curiosity.

media.title: A Link to the Past
media.description: Top-down perfection. Introduces the Light/Dark World split and the Master Sword. Foundational Zelda.
media.notes: ⭐ Essential. GBA port (Four Swords bundle) also recommended.

media.title: Link's Awakening (GB)
media.description: Link washes up on Koholint Island. Strange, dreamlike, no Zelda or Ganon. One of the best Game Boy games ever.
media.notes: Remake available on Switch (2019). Either version is great.

media.title: Zelda: The Wand of Gamelon
media.description: Infamous CD-i title featuring Zelda as the player character. Notorious for terrible animation and cutscenes.
media.notes: CD-i only — emulate via Nuvee/OpenMSX. Meme value only.

media.title: Link: The Faces of Evil
media.description: The other infamous CD-i Zelda. Source of the 'MMMM' and 'You must die!' memes. Historically significant for all the wrong reasons.
media.notes: CD-i only. Non-canon curiosity.

media.title: Ocarina of Time
media.description: 3D Zelda perfected. Child/Adult Link, Z-targeting, six sages. Widely considered one of the greatest games ever made.
media.notes: ⭐ Essential. GC version (Master Quest) or Citra (3DS remake) recommended over N64.

media.title: Majora's Mask
media.description: Three-day loop, 24 masks, existential dread. The darkest Zelda. Built on OoT's engine in 1.5 years.
media.notes: 3DS remake is excellent — adds saving improvements. GC version also fine.

media.title: Oracle of Ages
media.description: Time-travel puzzle-focused Zelda for GBC. Pairs with Oracle of Seasons — link passwords unlock a combined ending.
media.notes: Play both oracles together for the full story.

media.title: Oracle of Seasons
media.description: Action-focused companion to Ages — change seasons to solve puzzles. Rod of Seasons is a satisfying mechanic.
media.notes: Play alongside Ages. Link them for the true ending.

media.title: Four Swords
media.description: Multiplayer-only Zelda bundled with ALttP GBA. Required multiple GBAs and link cables. Notoriously hard to experience legitimately.
media.notes: Skip unless you have a group setup. Non-canonical spinoff feel.

media.title: The Wind Waker
media.description: Cell-shaded sailing adventure across a flooded Hyrule. Controversial at launch, now beloved. Gorgeous art direction.
media.notes: ⭐ WiiU HD version recommended — fixes the Triforce shard fetch quest significantly.

media.title: Four Swords Adventures
media.description: Single-player (or 4-player) overhead Zelda. Uses GBA as controller for handheld screens. Non-standard gameplay loop.
media.notes: Optional. Interesting experiment but not essential.

media.title: The Minish Cap
media.description: Shrink to Minish size to solve puzzles. Capcom-developed, charming and underrated. Introduces Ezlo the talking cap.
media.notes: Great GBA Zelda — well worth playing.

media.title: Twilight Princess
media.description: Dark, grounded Hyrule. Link transforms into a wolf. Midna is the best partner in the series. Long but excellent.
media.notes: ⭐ WiiU HD version fixes some pacing. GC version has correct (non-mirrored) world.

media.title: Phantom Hourglass
media.description: Wind Waker sequel on DS. Touch-screen controls, sailing, the Ocean King temple. Divisive due to repetitive dungeon.
media.notes: Worth playing as WW follow-up. Touch controls hold up reasonably.

media.title: Spirit Tracks
media.description: Train instead of a boat. Zelda is a ghost who possesses Phantom armor. Unique partner mechanic. Charming DS entry.
media.notes: Underrated. Zelda's role makes it memorable.

media.title: Skyward Sword
media.description: The earliest point in the Zelda timeline. Motion controls, Fi as companion. Story-heavy. Switch HD version removes motion requirement.
media.notes: Switch HD (2021) is the definitive version — button controls fix the divisive motion gameplay.

media.title: A Link Between Worlds
media.description: ALttP sequel — Link can merge into walls as a painting. Non-linear item rental system. One of the best 3DS games.
media.notes: ⭐ Essential. Play after ALttP for full effect.

media.title: Tri Force Heroes
media.description: 3-player co-op Zelda. Stack Links on each other's shoulders. Designed around multiplayer — solo play is awkward.
media.notes: Skip unless you have a group. Online servers still functional as of 2024.

media.title: Breath of the Wild
media.description: Open-world Zelda revolution. Climb anything, cook anything, approach everything your own way. 120 shrines, 900 Korok seeds.
media.notes: ⭐ Essential. Switch version is definitive. DLC (Champion's Ballad) adds a tough final dungeon.

media.title: Hyrule Warriors: Age of Calamity
media.description: BotW prequel musou — the Great Calamity 100 years prior. Not a mainline Zelda but fills in BotW backstory.
media.notes: Good if you enjoyed BotW's story. Musou gameplay — very different from mainline.

media.title: Tears of the Kingdom
media.description: BotW sequel. Ultrahand, Fuse, Ascend, Recall abilities. Sky islands, underground Depths, and a transformed Hyrule.
media.notes: ⭐ Essential. Best on Switch OLED. One of the highest-rated games ever made.

media.title: Echoes of Wisdom
media.description: First mainline game with Zelda as the protagonist. Copy-and-place 'echoes' mechanic. ALBW-style top-down.
media.notes: New 2024 release. First true Zelda-as-playable mainline entry.

media.title: Link's Awakening (Switch)
media.description: Full remake of the GB classic with toybox art style. Adds Chamber Dungeons mode. Faithful and beautiful.
media.notes: Recommended over the original for first-timers.

media.title: Ocarina of Time 3D
media.description: Near-perfect remake with visual overhaul, rearranged Water Temple, and Master Quest included.
media.notes: Best way to play OoT today. Emulate via Citra.

media.title: Majora's Mask 3D
media.description: Faithful 3DS remake adding a save system and rebalanced bosses. Some purists prefer the N64 original.
media.notes: Citra emulation works well. Recommended for newcomers.

media.title: Hyrule Warriors
media.description: Musou crossover featuring Zelda timeline characters. Zelda, Impa, Midna, Agitha all playable. Non-canon fanservice.
media.notes: Definitive Edition on Switch includes all DLC.

media.title: Cadence of Hyrule
media.description: Crypt of the NecroDancer crossover. Rhythm-based dungeon crawling in Hyrule. Charming indie collaboration.
media.notes: Great short experience. Unique rhythm mechanic.

media.title: Zelda: The Wand of Gamelon / Faces of Evil (cartoon cutscenes)
media.description: The infamous CD-i animated cutscenes. Meme origin point for the entire YouTube Poop era.
media.notes: Watch on YouTube — not worth emulating the full games just for these.

media.title: The Legend of Zelda (Netflix — canceled)
media.description: Nintendo and Netflix were reportedly developing a live-action Zelda series, described as 'Game of Thrones for families.' Canceled before announcement.
media.notes: Never produced. Listed as franchise history curiosity.

media.title: Akira Himekawa Zelda Manga (various)
media.description: Official manga adaptations of OoT, MM, TWW, TP, SS, ALttP, and others by duo Akira Himekawa. High quality Nintendo-approved comics.
media.notes: Published by Viz Media in English. Collected in box sets.
